title: clickable-diagram widget params
description: A shared widget module driving multiple diagram widgets. Dispatches on the `interaction` field between three modes: 'readout-only' (select + DATA-dictionary readout; drives w-univ and w-mon), 'proof-stepper' (select + nav buttons + SVG diagram of objects + morphisms; drives w-proof), and 'svg-diagram' (the select-diagram family: one or more controls driving a bespoke SVG + readout redraw; absorbs a family of ~50 widgets whose script bodies are widget-specific but whose chrome is shared). Portable consumers can ignore the *Literal artifact fields and render from the structured `cases` / `objects` / `morphisms` / `controls` data; the literals exist so renderScript can byte-match the legacy inline source.

titleTag.description: Tag used for the .ttl header element inside .hd. Default 'div' emits <div class="ttl">; 'span' emits <span class="ttl"> (a layout sub-variant in some legacy topics).

hintTag.description: Tag used for the .hint header element inside .hd. Default 'div'; 'span' pairs with titleTag='span' in the span-header layout sub-variant.

cases.description: Portable data: keys are option values; each entry is an object of string fields consumed by the template.

dataLiteral.description: ARTIFACT. Verbatim JS object-literal text for `const DATA = <here>;` in the driving IIFE. Preserves the original source's manual alignment (e.g. irregular spacing after keys). A portable consumer regenerates from `cases` and ignores this.

templateLiteral.description: ARTIFACT. Verbatim JS text for the right-hand side of `out.textContent = <here>;` — the backtick-literal chain that formats the readout. Preserves original newlines and indentation.

trailingExplainer.description:
Optional

...

rendered after the readout. Inner HTML.

hdHtml.description:
ARTIFACT. When present, overrides the default
...
line builder; the renderer emits `
${hdHtml}
\n` verbatim. Use for multi-line .hd variants that the default titleTag/hintTag + one-line template can't reproduce.

titleTag.description: Tag used for the .ttl header element inside .hd. Default 'div'; 'span' for the span-title layout sub-variant.

hintTag.description: Tag used for the .hint header element inside .hd. Default 'div'; 'span' pairs with titleTag='span' in the span-header layout sub-variant.

svgWidthAttr.description: ARTIFACT. Raw width attr value — may be a % or px literal.

svgStyleAttr.description: ARTIFACT. Raw style attr value for the <svg>.

outputLogStyleAttr.description: ARTIFACT. Raw style attr value for the log readout div.

initialKey.description: Option value the IIFE starts on (`let key = '…'`).

cases.description: Portable data: keys are option values; each entry carries { title, intro, nodes, given, steps } describing one proof.

proofsLiteral.description: ARTIFACT. Verbatim JS object-literal text for `const PROOFS = <here>;`. Preserves the original source's alignment (e.g. `x: 130, y:  80` with double space) and inline comments.

trailingExplainer.description:
Optional

...

rendered after the readout. Inner HTML.

hdHtml.description:
ARTIFACT. When present, overrides the default
...
line builder; the renderer emits `
${hdHtml}
\n` verbatim. Use for multi-line .hd variants that the default titleTag/hintTag + one-line template can't reproduce.

svgDiagram.description: The 'select-diagram' family: a <select> (or other control) + an SVG canvas + a readout div. On change of the control, the script redraws the SVG and rewrites the readout. Because each absorbed widget's draw/compute body is bespoke (different FNS tables, different DATA dictionaries, different plotting logic), the script body is carried verbatim as an artifact. The chrome — widget wrapper, header, row of controls, SVG attrs, readout div — is reconstructed from structured fields so that a portable consumer (React / SSR) can render sensible UI without touching the script body.

titleTag.description: Tag used for the .ttl header element inside .hd. Default 'div'; 'span' for the span-title layout sub-variant.

hintTag.description: Tag used for the .hint header element inside .hd. Default 'div'; 'span' pairs with titleTag='span' in the span-header layout sub-variant.

svgWidthAttr.description: ARTIFACT. Raw width attr value on <svg> — typically a px number or '100%'.

svgHeightAttr.description: ARTIFACT. Raw height attr value on <svg>.

svgTitle.description: Text inside the <svg>'s inner <title> element — usually the widget title, sometimes with manual HTML-entity escaping.

layout.description: Where the SVG sits relative to the controls row. 'controls-first' = row above SVG (the common case, e.g. w-multcalc, w-res). 'svg-first' = SVG above row (e.g. w-cubic, where the canvas is the primary visual and the controls tune it).

controlsLiteral.description: ARTIFACT. Verbatim inner HTML of the <div class="row"> block — the full sequence of labels, selects, range inputs, span readouts, and buttons with their original whitespace and indentation. A portable consumer who wants to render real React controls should ignore this and use the structured `controls` array (if present).

scriptBodyLiteral.description: ARTIFACT. Verbatim content of the driving <script> block, excluding the <script>/</script> tags themselves. Includes the leading '(function(){' and trailing '})();' and the newline after them. Preserves original alignment (e.g. aligned `const` declarations, comment columns).

controls.description: Optional portable description of the controls, parallel to controlsLiteral. Each entry describes one control. A React consumer can generate real inputs from this; the vanilla renderer ignores it and emits controlsLiteral verbatim.

cases.description: Optional portable data dictionary keyed by select-value. Each entry is an opaque payload the widget's draw/compute consumes — shape varies per widget. Present mostly as documentation for React consumers; the vanilla renderer drives everything through scriptBodyLiteral.

Optional

rendered after the readout. Inner HTML.
